sections.paragraph:
θησαυρός, οῦ, ὁ (s. prec. entry; Hes., Hdt.+; loanw. in rabb.; τὴν θ. TestSol 13:2 C).

① a place where someth. is kept for safekeeping, repository (Theoph. Ant. 2, 35 [p. 188, 16])

ⓐ of repositories for material things

α. treasure box or chest (cp. X., An. 5, 4, 27; Diod S 17, 71, 1; PTebt 6, 27; PAmh 41, 8; En 97:9; Jos., Ant. 9, 163) Mt 2:11.

β. storehouse, storeroom (Appian, Fgm. 6; PRyl 231, 8; PFay 122, 4; POxy 101, 29; Am 8:5; Pr 8:21) Mt 13:52 (with ἐκβάλλειν ἐκ τοῦ θ. cp. Arrian, Cyneg. 34, 1 ἐμβάλλειν ἐς τὸν θ.).

ⓑ of repositories for transcendent things (TestAsh 1:9) of the heart as the treasury for heavenly possessions θ. τῆς καρδίας Lk 6:45; cp. Mt 12:35 (on ἀγαθὸς θ. s. Dt 28:12).—GKaminski, JDAI 106, ’91, 63–181 (ins); s. SEG XLI, 1846.

② that which is stored up, treasure Mt 6:21; Lk 12:34 (cp. Just., A I, 15, 16).

ⓐ of material things (TestSol C 10, 21 al.; Diod S 17, 71, 1) Mt 6:19; 13:44 (for θ. buried in the ground: Maximus Tyr. 15, 5h; Artem. 2, 58; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 6, 39 p. 250, 4ff; JDerrett, Law in the NT, ’70, 1–16). The treasures of Egypt Hb 11:26.

ⓑ of transcendent things (Iren. 4, 26, 1 [Harv. II 235, 9]; on θ. that is dug up in metaph. sense cp. Philemo Com. 169 Κ. ἐὰν γυνὴ γυναικὶ κατʼ ἰδίαν ὁμιλεῖ, μεγάλων κακῶν θησαυρὸς ἐξορύσσεται. Metaph. use also bγ end)

α. of the treasures in heaven Mt 6:20. θ. ἀνέκλειπτος ἐν τ. οὐρανοῖς Lk 12:33. θησαυρὸν ἔχειν ἐν οὐρανῷ have treasure in heaven the treasure is, in effect, deposited there and becomes available to people after death Mt 19:21; Mk 10:21; Lk 18:22.—WPesch, Biblica 41, ’60, 356–78 (Mt 6:19–21; Lk 12:33f).

β. θησαυροὶ σκοτεινοί treasures lying in darkness B 11:4 (Is 45:3).

γ. of the gospel and its glory 2 Cor 4:7. Of Christ: ἐν ᾧ εἰσιν πάντες οἱ θησαυροὶ τῆς σοφίας καὶ γνώσεως ἀπόκρυφοι in whom all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge lie hidden Col 2:3 (ἐν θησαυροῖς σοφίας Sir 1:25; cp. X., Mem. 1, 6, 14; 4, 2, 9 θησαυροὶ σοφίας; Pla., Phileb. 15e; Diod S 9, 10, 3 κάλλιστος θ.; Himerius, Or. [Ecl.] 3, 20 θ. ἀθάνατος of the possession of virtue; Pr 2:3ff; Philo, Congr. Erud. Gr. 127).—B. 777; BHHW III 1686. DELG. M-M. EDNT. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

someth. someth. = something

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

TestAsh TestAsh = Testament of Asher, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JDAI JDAI = Jahrbuch des deutschen archäologischen Instituts—Lists 6

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

NT NT = New Testament

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Biblica Biblica (unabbr.)—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 456
